top_headlines.headline: IDX Closed for Pancasila Day — Last Trade May 29
top_headlines.summary: Indonesian markets are closed Monday June 1 for Pancasila Day. The last session saw broad selling across materials and consumer names. Market reopens Tuesday June 2.

top_headlines.headline: Rupiah Hits 17,878 — Nearing 52-Week High
top_headlines.summary: USD/IDR at 17,878, approaching the 52-week peak of 17,899. Purbaya maintains confidence but external pressures from Middle East tensions and trade uncertainty persist.

top_headlines.headline: Oil Supply Chain Damage May Be Permanent Despite US-Iran Thaw
top_headlines.summary: Strait of Hormuz disruptions have caused lasting logistical damage to global oil supply. Brent crude holds above $93. Recovery could take months even with geopolitical de-escalation.

top_headlines.headline: Gold Pulls Back 0.8% to $4,555 — Profit-Taking After Record Run
top_headlines.summary: Gold retreated from record highs but remains elevated. Strong support from safe-haven demand continues on Middle East uncertainty and trade policy shifts.

top_headlines.headline: US Futures Positive After Record May Close
top_headlines.summary: S&P futures +0.26%, Nasdaq +0.49%, VIX below 15. Markets carry May momentum into June. Key focus: May jobs report this Friday.

ADRO.headline: ADRO Flat at 2,300 — Awaiting Renewables Pivot

PTBA.headline: PTBA Off 2.11% to 2,780 — Export Policy Headwind

IKBI.headline: IKBI Down 2.91% to 500 — Small-Cap Outflow Victim
